subject: Human Resources
title: Job Evaluation Methods
description: A 60-second lesson on Job Evaluation Methods.

hook.text: Two employees with the same title do completely different work. One makes $30K more. How did that happen?

buildup.text: Job evaluation systematically determines the relative worth of each role—based on skills, effort, responsibility, and conditions.

discovery.text: Common methods include ranking, classification, point-factor analysis, and market pricing—each with trade-offs.

twist.text: Without formal evaluation, pay is set by negotiation power—which favors the loudest voices, not the hardest workers.

climax.text: Point-factor systems score jobs on objective criteria—creating defensible, consistent pay structures across the organization.

punchline.text: Pay should follow the role's value, not who negotiated better.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of job evaluation?
options: To systematically determine the relative worth of each role
To decide who gets promoted first
